messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Which philosopher is traditionally credited as the author of the Symposium?

The Symposium (會飲篇) is traditionally attributed to Plato. It is one of his celebrated dialogues, presenting a series of speeches on the nature of love (eros) set at a banquet in ancient Athens. While Socrates appears as a central character and voice within the dialogue, Socrates himself wrote nothing that has survived — he left no extant texts. The Symposium, like other Platonic dialogues, is Plato's composition, not Socrates', Confucius', Laozi's, or Mencius'.
